title: Simulate an LRU cache
prompt_md:
Implement `simulate_lru(capacity, accesses)`.

Process each key in `accesses` in order. Each access inserts a new key or refreshes an existing one (marks it most-recently-used). When the number of distinct cached keys would exceed `capacity`, evict the **least-recently-used** key.

Return the list of keys currently cached, ordered **least-recent first** (most-recent last).

Example: `simulate_lru(2, ["a", "b", "a", "c"])` -> `["a", "c"]`. (After a,b the cache is [a,b]; accessing a refreshes it to [b,a]; adding c evicts b -> [a,c].)
hints: Keep an ordered list of keys. On access, remove the key if present, then append it to the end (most-recent).
After appending, if len exceeds capacity, pop from the front (index 0) — that's the least-recently-used.
